author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Decision: Rather than writing a fused CUDA kernel (which would break autograd), the biggest win is eliminating the 6 tensor splits per timestep. Currently splits [batch, 3*hs] → 6 separate [batch, hs] tensors by copying data. Instead, use narrow() (zero-copy from CL-261) to create views into the concatenated tensor. This eliminates 6 allocations + 6 memcpys per timestep.

Also transpose the weights once outside the seq loop instead of per-timestep.
